display.purpose: Hero trip titles, set as a cinematic title sequence over the photograph.

body.purpose: Monospaced long-form copy; the field-journal voice. Cap measures near 90ch.

label.purpose: Tracked uppercase eyebrows, field labels, metadata keys, chips. Short phrases only.

motion.name: ease-cinematic
motion.purpose: House ease-out. Hero cross-fades, reveals, and CTA transitions all use it.

motion.name: hero-crossfade
motion.purpose: Trip image swap behind the hero title sequence.

motion.name: hero-pan
motion.purpose: Slow 1.04x scale pan on the active hero image.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-in reveal. NOTE: currently gates content visibility; needs a reduced-motion + no-JS visible default.

motion.name: cta-invert
motion.purpose: Ghost button fill inversion on hover.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Cream-on-ink submit for the waitlist and booking modal.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outlined 'Request to book' CTA on paper; inverts to solid ink on hover.

components.name: Interest Chip
components.description: Multi-select interest toggle on the ink waitlist ground.

components.name: Underline Field
components.description: Boxless field: cream mono text over a single bottom rule, tracked uppercase label above.

components.name: Cinematic Trip Title
components.description: Signature hero list item: serif title with italic obsession word, mono superscript date.

narrative.overview: freaks reads like the opening frames of an A24 film bound into a serious obsessive's logbook. The mood is cinematic: a full-bleed, grayscale-leaning photograph fills the first viewport, dimmed under a vignette and a film-grain overlay, with the trip names set as a hovering title sequence over the frame. Then the page settles into the field-journal half of its identity, warm paper stock, hairline rules, monospaced detail set in the cadence of a runner's training log. The serif supplies the cinema; the mono supplies the documentation. The system makes the right person recognize themselves and the wrong person move on: it states the bar plainly and trusts the reader to react.
keyCharacteristics: Cinematic full-bleed hero, photograph as the design, grain and vignette doing the mood work
Two-temperature palette: warm paper by day, near-black ink by night, one terracotta voice
Serif display (cinema) against monospaced body (field notes), no third typeface
Sharp corners, hairline rules, no shadows, no cards
Specificity as luxury: real names, real times, real benchmarks
One obsession per surface; long scroll, deliberate pacing

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only chroma in the system and appears on a sliver of any screen: links, the mark, selection. Everything else is paper or ink. Never introduce a second accent hue.

rules.name: The Two-Temperature Rule
rules.body: Every section is either paper (daylight, documentary) or ink (night, cinema). There is no in-between gray surface. Transitions are hard cuts across a hairline rule, never gradients.

rules.name: The Italic-Obsession Rule
rules.body: In display and title type, the thing you're obsessed with is serif italic and the place is roman. Italics mark the obsession, not arbitrary emphasis.

rules.name: The Caps-Are-Labels-Only Rule
rules.body: Uppercase is reserved for short tracked labels, eyebrows, and metadata keys. Body copy is never caps; mono body never goes above weight 300.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat. If something needs to feel separate, change its temperature (paper vs. ink) or rule it off, never drop a shadow. Grain and vignette are atmosphere, not elevation.

dos: Do keep terracotta to a sliver (links, mark, selection). Its scarcity is the brand.
Do set every surface as either warm paper or near-black ink, separated by hairline rules.
Do set body copy in IBM Plex Mono weight 300 and cap measures near 90ch.
Do mark the obsession in serif italic and the place in roman.
Do lead with specificity: name the expert, the hotel, the trail, the exact time.
Do ship real cinematic photography full-bleed; the photograph is the design.
Do keep corners at 0px and surfaces flat.
donts: Don't build a generic travel-operator layout: card grids, star ratings, 'book now' clutter, stock-smile photography.
Don't drift into hustle/retreat-bro wellness: countdown timers, neon gradients, transformation promises.
Don't fall into corporate SaaS scaffolding: feature grids, big-number hero metrics, gradient-on-white, buzzword copy.
Don't chase mass-luxury influencer travel: over-filtered aspiration, conspicuous logos, FOMO marketing.
Don't add a second accent hue or a third typeface; two families, one chroma.
Don't use em dashes in copy; en dashes for numeric ranges only.
Don't set placeholder or label text so light it fails 4.5:1.
Don't round corners, drop shadows, or use a card.
